heaviest_rainfall.status: The most intense rainfall recorded took place on 09 November 2025, with precipitation totalling 47.89mm.  The rain fell at 24.6mm per hour at times over the course of the 144.8 hour storm.

heaviest_rainrate.status: On 02 September 2025, the rain fell at an exceptional rate, peaking at 34.3mm per hour, the heaviest downpour recorded.  A total of 21.19mm fell over the 15.0 hour storm.

longest_rain.status: The lengthiest precipitation event recorded spanned 144.8 hours on 09 November 2025, it was characterized by a peak rain intensity of 24.6mm per hour and a total rainfall of 47.89mm.

most_lightning.status: The lightning strike count peaked at 23 on 07 September 2025, the highest since recording started on 11 April 2025.

storm.status: There is a storm in the vicinity.  This is the first storm since 02 January 2026.

top_10_dry.status: The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 26 November 2025 and lasted for 14 days.

cold_snaps.status: There is currently a cold snap underway.  It started on 2026-01-01 and has lasted 6 day.  So far this year, there has been 1 cold snap.  Typically, the yearly average is 3.6.

extreme.status: The highest recorded temperature difference is 26.7°C from 29 April 2025 (when the temperature reached 27.1°C but fell to 0.4°C).

heatwaves.status: There is currently no heatwaves.  This year there have been 0 heatwavess, usually there are 1.9 heatwavess per year.

tropical.status: The last observed tropical night was 18 July 2025, making a total of only 62 times since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is southerly (195°).  In the UK, a southerly wind usually brings milder, moist air from the Atlantic.

gusts.status: To date, the maximum wind gust recorded is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

run.status: The highest wind run ever seen is 1737 miles on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The largest wind speed ever was on 13 October 2012 with a value of 93.7 mph.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the outside temperature (down 2.8°C), feels like temperature (down 2.8°C), and inside temperature (down 2.5°C) and more moisture-laden rainfall (up 0.1 mm).

The past week and month had observable variation in dew point temperature (up 4.0°C), inside temperature (up 5.8°C), and outside temperature (up 4.4°C) when compared to today.

The cloud levels for yesterday ranged from 845m to 1717m and averaged 1107.7m, while the past week and month indicated warmer conditions.

summary.summary: Inside; anticipate brisk, cold temperatures. Outside; extremely cold temperatures will prevail. Also, the atmosphere will feel parched and dry and little or no wind. Possible light showers, cloud cover will be variable, overcast at times, and light levels will be low during the day. Little change in the weather.

washing.phrase: The current temperature is too low (2.9°C) for optimal drying conditions.  Any drying will take longer.
